generated_by: experimental/scripts/l1_t7_atlas.py (atlas) + experimental/scripts/verify_l1_t7_atlas.py (concurrency law / witness cross-checks, independent reimplementation)
description: Compact certificate for the T=7 candidate atlas and the fat-tail concurrency law. Does NOT include the full 23314-shape atlas enumeration (regenerate with experimental/scripts/l1_t7_atlas.py); carries per-ell counts, the empirical concurrency-law table, the ell=31 witness in full, and the coverage ledger.

atlas_notes.j_plus_5_theorem: over-determination := sum_k(mu_k-1) - (ell-2) = j+5 identically on every excess=3 shape (0 violations across all 23314 shapes, all five ell); see note Sec 1 for the proof.
atlas_notes.min_j_family: [a, ell-a, 9] for ceil(ell/2) <= a <= ell-9 realizes the cap-tight (capslack=0) min-j shapes for ell>=19; ell=17 has no j=3 solution (ell-9=8 < 9) so min_j=4 there. min_j_cap_tight_count equals the width of that a-range exactly (1,3,6,7 for ell=19,23,29,31).

concurrency_law.source: internal 'Lane I' hunt (tally.py/sweep_tally*.json/sweep_deep.json; not shipped verbatim -- see verify_l1_t7_atlas.py gate v for an independent live reproduction of the ell=17,p=137 row (full 680/680) and a bounded 40-dropset subsample at ell=31,p=373)

cross_reference_pr364.note: PR #364's own m*(19)<=9 listing witness (ell=19,p=571) is independently an instance of the SAME [ell-3,3^k] fat-tail family this note studies (its spectrum [16,3^6,2^6,1^17] has mu_1=16=ell-3 and six size-3 fibers): k3=6, matching this table's (ell=19,p=571) row exactly. Two unrelated methods (PR #364's listing search; this note's Lane I concurrency tally, at a DIFFERENT dropset) converge on the identical extremal k3=6 at that prime. Cited by path+number, not depended on -- re-verified from PR #364's own embedded gamma in verify_l1_t7_atlas.py gate v.

pairplant_balanced_shape_coverage.source: internal 'Lane I' hunt (pairplant.py/pairplant_sweep.json); balanced two-big-fiber plants, d=ell+1-b1-b2 in {2,3}
pairplant_balanced_shape_coverage.note: no run realized a size-9 third fiber; the minimal-j frontier [a,ell-a,9] was not reached by any tractable plant of this kind (its cap-tight regime has zero spare nullspace dimension to sweep).

ell31_witness.independent_rederivation: verify_l1_t7_atlas.py gate iii reconstructs gamma from (ell,p,dropset,q) alone by exact polynomial division/multiplication, then recomputes spectrum by brute-force evaluation AND concurrency by an exhaustive triple-tally that is not given q -- both reproduce every field above exactly.

coverage_ledger.fat_tail_family: [ell-3,3^k]: exhaustive-per-dropset triple tally (every triple in every non-planted coset solved), dropsets themselves sampled (deterministic spread, exhaustive only at ell=17,p=137 with 680/680 = C(17,3)). 17 (ell,p) points across ell in {17,19,23,29,31}, up to 1200 dropsets/prime.
coverage_ledger.balanced_dlt3_family: pairplant.py: d=ell+1-b1-b2 in {2,3} (pencil/plane, exhaustively swept once planted), ell in {17,19,23}, 10 plant configurations -- thin coverage, no size-9 third fiber ever emerged.
coverage_ledger.minimal_j_frontier_NOT_covered: the [a,ell-a,9] family (capslack=0, j=3) has ZERO spare nullspace dimension once both top fibers are planted cap-tight; the alternative (plant only the size-9 third fiber) leaves an (ell-9)-dimensional projective family, intractable to sweep exhaustively for ell>=19. No tractable method exists yet -- this is the open frontier the note names, not a gap in effort.
coverage_ledger.not_claimed: C'<=2 is not proved; k3<=7 is not proved (EXPERIMENTAL, finite but real coverage); the atlas is complete only under the two named combinatorial caps (pairwise + Lemma R), not a completeness statement about realizability.
